name: Napa Valley Chicken Wings with Wine Dressing
recipeIngredient: 1/2 medium Green bell pepper, thinly sliced crosswise
recipeInstructions:
1. Remove chicken wings from their bones and discard the tips.
2. Press the flesh of the remaining wing portions to one end of the bone.
3. With a sharp knife, remove any smaller bones in the wing portion containing two bones.
4. Flatten the fleshy ends of the chicken pieces so they will stand upright.
5. Mix cornstarch with 2 teaspoons salt and white pepper.
6. Dredge the chicken in the mixture and set aside to dry for 30 minutes.
7. Heat oil to a depth of 1/2 inch in a heavy skillet.
8. Fry the chicken until golden brown and tender, about 7 minutes on each side.
9. Drain on paper towels and refrigerate or freeze if not to be used immediately.
10. To make the dressing, combine olive oil, tarragon wine vinegar, dry white wine, mashed garlic clove, dry mustard, sugar, dried basil, dried oregano, and dried tarragon in a bowl.
11. Season with salt and pepper to taste.
12. Blend well until smooth.
13. Combine tomato slices, green bell pepper slices, and onion slices with the dressing and mix well.
14. To serve, bring chicken wings to room temperature and arrange upright over tomatoes in a shallow casserole.
